title: Inter-agent message sanitization: prompt injection circuit breaker
description: Add a `_content_looks_suspicious()` guard in the inter-agent message passing layer (notes_bridge or task dispatch) that scans content flowing from one agent to another for prompt injection patterns before it is forwarded. This mirrors the existing `_content_looks_like_error()` guard on Substack publishing, applied to the lateral channel between agents.
rationale: The reading note crystallizes the mechanism behind lateral agent-to-agent corruption: unlike traditional supply chain attacks that require planting malicious content at a single upstream contact point, agent corruption can propagate dynamically through inter-agent message passing — no human intermediary needed. Mira already has a circuit breaker for outbound publishing (`_content_looks_like_error()`) and for imported skills (`soul_manager.audit_skill()`), but the inter-agent communication channel (notes_inbox/notes_outbox, task output routing in core.py) has no equivalent guard. A compromised or manipulated agent (e.g., explorer fetching a poisoned feed, or writer processing adversarial content) could inject override instructions into the next agent's inbox. The reading note's open question — 'are there natural circuit breakers?' — maps directly to this gap in Mira's architecture.
diff_description: In `agents/shared/notes_bridge.py` (or wherever agent task outputs are read and forwarded), add a function `_content_looks_suspicious(text: str) -> bool` that checks for prompt injection signatures: phrases like 'ignore previous instructions', 'you are now', 'system:', 'new directive', '[INST]', or large blocks of ALL-CAPS imperative text. In `agents/super/core.py`, call this check before any agent output is placed into another agent's inbox or used as input to a downstream task. If flagged: log the content hash + source agent, quarantine the message (write to a `notes_quarantine/` directory instead of `notes_inbox/`), and alert via the normal error reporting path. Do not silently drop — quarantine preserves forensic value. This creates a natural circuit breaker: single-agent compromise cannot propagate to the full mesh without passing the sanitization layer.
